back: A subset of ℝⁿ is compact if and only if it is closed and bounded.

front: In which specific metric space does the standard Heine-Borel Theorem hold?
back: The theorem holds in ℝⁿ with the standard Euclidean metric.

front: Does the Heine-Borel Theorem generalize to all metric spaces?
back: No, it does not. In a general metric space, closed and bounded does not necessarily imply compact.

front: What is the significance of the Heine-Borel Theorem in real analysis?
back: It provides a simple and practical characterization of compact sets in Euclidean space, linking the topological property of compactness to the easier-to-verify properties of being closed and bounded.

front: State the two implications that make up the Heine-Borel Theorem.
back:
1. If a subset of ℝⁿ is compact, then it is closed and bounded.
2. If a subset of ℝⁿ is closed and bounded, then it is compact.
